name: perigon-angular
description: Angular 21+ standalone/Material/signal conventions for Perigon WebApp
capabilities: Define Angular Material theming and styling conventions
Recommend signal-based state management and forms
Structure routes, services, and API client patterns
Organize i18n string files and translation keys
Configure auth guards and HTTP interceptors
author: AterDev
securitySummary: All 22 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This skill contains only documentation describing Angular 21+ development conventions. The pre-computed security audit explicitly states: 'This skill contains only documentation in markdown format. No code execution, file system access, network calls, or external commands are present.' The static scanner misidentified SHA-256 content hashes, GitHub repository URLs, and development documentation references as security threats.
valueStatement: This skill provides standardized Angular 21+ development conventions for Perigon WebApp. It covers component architecture, state management with signals, and Material Design integration to help developers maintain consistent, modern code.
limitations: Skill does not generate code or modify files directly
No build or test execution capabilities
No access to project files for automated updates

useCases.title: Angular project conventions
useCases.description: Apply consistent project structure and coding patterns across Angular 21+ Perigon WebApp development

useCases.title: Service and API patterns
useCases.description: Set up HTTP clients, interceptors, and environment configurations following Perigon standards

useCases.title: Material Design implementation
useCases.description: Implement Angular Material components with proper theming and standalone import patterns

promptTemplates.title: New page setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Angular 21+ page component using Perigon conventions. Include standalone setup, signal-based state, Material imports, and proper route configuration in the pages directory.

promptTemplates.title: Service implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement an Angular service following Perigon patterns. Use the base service pattern, include proper type definitions, configure HTTP interceptor, and reference environment configurations.

promptTemplates.title: Theming setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up Angular Material theming for a new feature using Perigon conventions. Include theme file structure, color palette setup, and standalone Material import patterns.

promptTemplates.title: i18n integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Add new internationalization strings following Perigon conventions. Include i18n key definition, JSON file updates, and alignment with existing translation key patterns.

bestPractices: Use standalone components exclusively and avoid NgModules when possible
Prefer signals and signal forms over legacy two-way binding patterns
Keep component styles alongside their respective component files
antiPatterns: Avoid using deprecated NgModule patterns when standalone alternatives exist
Do not mix legacy two-way binding with signals unnecessarily
Avoid global style scattering - keep page styles with their components

faq.question: Which Angular versions are supported?
faq.answer: This skill supports Angular 21 and newer versions with full signal and standalone component support.

faq.question: Can this skill modify my project files?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance and conventions only. It does not generate or modify files directly.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests or builds?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not execute any build commands, tests, or external tooling operations.

faq.question: How do I integrate with existing Perigon code?
faq.answer: Follow the directory structure and import patterns described in the skill. Match existing conventions in src/ClientApp/WebApp.

faq.question: What if my project uses different naming conventions?
faq.answer: The skill provides Perigon-specific standards. Adapt the principles to match your project requirements where needed.

faq.question: How does this compare to general Angular guides?
faq.answer: This skill provides Perigon-specific project structure, tooling preferences, and team conventions rather than general Angular documentation.
